message.content:
Research the following about Google NotebookLM:
1. Can NotebookLM export or generate PowerPoint (.pptx) files? Does it have any PPT generation feature?
2. Does NotebookLM provide infographic suggestions or visual layout recommendations?
3. Is there any API available for NotebookLM that could be used programmatically?
4. What are the main outputs NotebookLM can generate (audio, text summaries, etc.)?
5. Are there any workarounds or integrations that allow using NotebookLM output to feed into PPT generation?

Please search the web for recent information (2024-2025) about these capabilities.

content.text: I'll search for current information about Google NotebookLM's capabilities, focusing on PowerPoint integration, infographics, API availability, and output formats.

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of Google NotebookLM's PowerPoint PPTX export capabilities:

## Slide Deck Generation & PPTX Export

NotebookLM has evolved dramatically since 2025, adding exportable slide decks (now with PPTX export), infographics, video overviews, native note export to Google Docs.

### Timeline of PPTX Support

Google is rolling out PPTX export starting in February 2026, with PPTX export and per-slide editing now available as of February 2026. The Slide Deck feature was released around mid-to-late November 2025, initially available to Pro users, and has now been gradually rolled out to all users.

### How to Export as PPTX

To download the Slide Deck, select the three dots menu and "Download PDF Document (.pdf)" or "Download PowerPoint (.pptx)". The PPTX export lets you open directly in PowerPoint or import into Google Slides for further editing.

### Key Features

The slides include infographics, charts, Venn diagrams, and visuals that actually add context. Per-slide editing (new February 2026) allows you to revise individual slides using text prompts instead of regenerating the entire deck.

### Limitations

Exported PPTX files use AI-regenerated layouts, so they may not be pixel-perfect replicas of what you see in NotebookLM, and some elements may export as images rather than editable text boxes.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Google NotebookLM API programmatic access in 2024-2025:

## Official Google API

In September 2025, Google released the NotebookLM Enterprise API, enabling programmatic access to NotebookLM features for the first time. Users can perform notebook management tasks programmatically, including creating new notebooks and managing them through APIs.

The official API documentation shows that you can add various content types as data sources, including Google Docs, Google Slides, raw text, web content, and YouTube videos.

## Important Limitations

The NotebookLM API is currently in alpha, and some features may not work as documented due to API limitations.

## Community/Unofficial Solutions

Prior to the official API release, developers created workarounds:

- Developer teng-lin's notebooklm-py project, released in January 2025, reverse-engineers NotebookLM's undocumented APIs, giving users programmatic access to every feature the platform offers.

- AutoContent API provides full programmatic access to NotebookLM's features including AI-generated summaries, audio conversations, and study guides.

The official NotebookLM Enterprise API represents the first official programmatic access solution from Google, though third-party tools remain available as alternatives.

I've successfully performed a web search for your query. Here are the key findings about NotebookLM infographic features:

## Visual Layout Recommendations

NotebookLM allows you to select an orientation, such as Square, Portrait, or Landscape. The horizontal format (16:9) is ideal for LinkedIn posts, blog headers, and email campaigns; the vertical format (9:16) is perfect for Instagram Stories and mobile content; and the square format (1:1) enables cross-platform syndication.

## Customization Features

You can choose a level of detail from Concise, Standard, or Detailed (beta). NotebookLM offers 10 preset visual styles, each crafted to suit different scenarios, including "Professional," "Instructional," and "Kawaii".

## Key Style Presets

The Bento Grid provides an organized, sectioned layout resembling a bento box, and the Bricks style offers a blocky, Lego-inspired design that provides a playful and dynamic approach to visual storytelling. The Instructional style uses a step-by-step layout ideal for guides, tutorials, or processes.

## Core Purpose

Infographics turn complex information from your sources into a single, engaging visual summary designed to help you quickly understand main points, visualize data, or picture relationships between different concepts.

Based on the web search results, here's a comprehensive overview of NotebookLM's output formats, audio capabilities, and features:

## Audio Capabilities

Audio Overviews are deep-dive discussions between AI hosts that provide in-depth summaries of the key topics in your uploaded sources. The NotebookLM audio overview feature is an AI-powered tool that transforms your uploaded documents into engaging podcast-style discussions. Instead of robotic text-to-speech, it uses two AI hosts who analyze your content and create natural conversations.

### Audio Customization Options

In the customization panel, select Brief (2-3 minutes), Standard (5-6 minutes), or Extended (8-10 minutes). The audio overview notebook lm interface lets you tag sections for emphasis. Click priority areas like "Financial Analysis" or "Implementation Steps" for detailed coverage.

You can generate Audio Overviews in 80+ languages. You can join a conversation and interact with the AI hosts in Audio Overview. With your voice, you can ask the hosts for more details or to explain a concept differently.

## Text-Based Outputs

It creates documents like timelines, FAQs, briefing docs, summaries, and even study guides.

## Additional Output Formats

It's unique in being able to generate everything from audio and video to reports, slides and infographics from your source materials. A new interactive Mind Map feature lets users navigate complex topics, explore new connections, and gain deeper understanding of their materials.

## Download and Sharing

To download the Audio Overview, select Download. Audio Overviews can be downloaded for offline listening.

Based on the search results, here are the main workarounds and approaches for integrating NotebookLM with PowerPoint presentation generation:

## Direct Export to PowerPoint

As of February 18, 2026, NotebookLM now supports slide-level editing and PPTX export, allowing you to modify individual slides directly inside NotebookLM and export them as PowerPoint files. You can download the Slide Deck by selecting the three dots menu and choosing "Download PowerPoint (.pptx)".

## Converting PDF Slides to PowerPoint

If you're working with NotebookLM-generated PDF slides, there are several conversion methods:

1. **Using Codia AI NoteSlide**: Convert NotebookLM generated PDFs into editable PowerPoint presentations using Codia AI Vision. It precisely recreates original layouts, fonts, and styles into fully editable PowerPoint files, leverages AI Vision to intelligently detect text, graphics, and slide structure, and allows conversion in seconds.

2. **Using Canva**: Download your slides from NotebookLM as a PDF, upload the PDF to Canva (which automatically turns the pages into a presentation project), then download as Microsoft PowerPoint (.pptx) for a fully functional file that works in PowerPoint or Google Slides.

3. **Using OCR Tools**: Tenorshare PDNob is a faster, more automated approach that converts NotebookLM PDF slides directly into an editable PowerPoint using OCR features.

## Content Integration Workflow

For presentations with rich media, you can export PowerPoint files to device storage, upload the same to Google Slides, and add them to NotebookLM with a single click, which unlocks the powerful analytical capabilities of NotebookLM for all PowerPoint content.

content.text:
Based on my research, here's a comprehensive summary of Google NotebookLM's capabilities regarding your five questions:

## 1. PowerPoint (.pptx) Export & Generation

Yes, NotebookLM now has direct PowerPoint export capabilities as of February 2026. The platform generates slide decks natively, and you can download them directly as PPTX files by selecting the three-dot menu and choosing "Download PowerPoint (.pptx)". As of February 2026, you can also edit individual slides using text prompts before exporting, rather than regenerating the entire deck.

## 2. Infographic Suggestions & Visual Layout Recommendations

Yes, NotebookLM provides extensive visual customization options:
- **10 preset visual styles** including "Professional," "Instructional," "Kawaii," "Bento Grid," and "Bricks"
- **3 orientation options**: Square (1:1), Portrait (9:16), and Landscape (16:9) suited for different platforms
- **3 detail levels**: Concise, Standard, and Detailed (beta)
- **Visual elements**: Includes charts, Venn diagrams, infographics, and context-rich visuals that automatically generate based on your content

## 3. API Availability for Programmatic Use

Yes, there are API solutions available:
- **Official**: Google released the **NotebookLM Enterprise API** in September 2025 (currently in alpha), allowing programmatic access to notebook management, data source management, and other core features
- **Unofficial/Community**: The open-source **notebooklm-py** project (GitHub: teng-lin/notebooklm-py) provides full programmatic access to NotebookLM features including capabilities not exposed in the web UI
- **Third-party**: AutoContent API also provides programmatic access to NotebookLM features

## 4. Main Output Formats NotebookLM Can Generate

NotebookLM supports diverse output types:
- **Audio**: Audio Overviews (podcast-style AI discussions in 2-3 min, 5-6 min, or 8-10 min versions, available in 80+ languages)
- **Visual**: Slide decks (PPTX), infographics, video overviews, mind maps
- **Text-based**: Study guides, summaries, timelines, FAQs, briefing documents
- **Interactive**: Audio conversations where users can ask AI hosts for clarification

## 5. Workarounds & Integrations for PPT Generation

Since direct PPTX export is now available natively, workarounds are less critical, but alternatives include:
- **Codia AI NoteSlide**: Converts NotebookLM PDF slides into fully editable PowerPoint with AI Vision detecting text and graphics
- **Canva**: Upload NotebookLM PDFs to Canva, which auto-converts pages to a presentation, then export as PPTX
- **OCR Tools**: Tenorshare PDNob converts PDF slides to editable PowerPoint using OCR
- **Google Slides Integration**: Download as PDF, import to Google Slides, then convert to PowerPoint

Sources:
- [Export from NotebookLM: Complete Guide to Audio, Video, Slides & Research](https://exploreaitogether.com/export-download-notebooklm-guide/)
- [Generate a Slide Deck in NotebookLM - Official Help](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16757456?hl=en)
- [NotebookLM's Slide Decks finally got the two features it desperately needed](https://www.xda-developers.com/notebooklms-slide-decks-finally-got-two-features-it-desperately-needed/)
- [Generate an Infographic in NotebookLM - Official Help](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16758265?hl=en)
- [NotebookLM Visual Style Picker: How to Use the 10 Infographic Presets](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/notebooklm-infographic-styles/)
- [Create and manage notebooks (API) - NotebookLM Enterprise](https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/enterprise/notebooklm-enterprise/docs/api-notebooks)
- [GitHub - notebooklm-py: Unofficial Python API](https://github.com/teng-lin/notebooklm-py)
- [Generate Audio Overview in NotebookLM - Official Help](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16212820?hl=en)
- [Codia AI NoteSlide — Turn NotebookLM Slides into Fully Editable PowerPoint](https://codia.ai/noteslide)
- [4 Ways to Convert NotebookLM Slides to Editable PPT](https://www.pdnob.com/ocr/how-to-convert-notebooklm-pdf-slides-to-editable-powerpoint.html)
